title: ancistrocarya japonica
intro: Ancistrocarya japonica, also known as Japanese dodder, is a type of parasitic plant that belongs to the Apocynaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Ancistrocarya japonica include its ability to relieve fever, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the roots and stems in hot water or using them in a decoction, and are often used to treat a range of conditions, including respiratory issues and skin problems.
